zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Emma Wallis於1495年出生在英國英格蘭爾巴堂姆利時，她的父親Thomas Wallis37歲，母親Katheryn Hayward35歲。她於1508年與John Webster I在英國萊希斯特斯爾可希頓結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1558年3月20日在英國萊希斯特斯爾可希頓享年63歲，埋葬在英國萊希斯特斯爾可希頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Emma Wallis于1495年出生在英国英格兰爾巴堂姆利时，她的父亲Thomas Wallis37岁，母亲Katheryn Hayward35岁。她于1508年与John Webster I在英国萊希斯特斯爾可希頓结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1558年3月20日在英国萊希斯特斯爾可希頓享年63岁，埋葬在英国萊希斯特斯爾可希頓。

nameMeanings.name: Wallis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: from Anglo-Norman French Waleis, Walais ‘Welshman’, also sometimes ‘Breton’ (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning a ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). In western and central England the medieval reference is clearly to Welshmen. In Norfolk and Lincolnshire, the reference was probably to Bretons, many of whom settled in the eastern counties after the Conquest. Compare Welsh , Walsh . The idea that the Scottish surname refers to the Welsh-speaking Britons of Strathclyde is erroneous.

variants.name: Wallace
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Wallis  , especially in Scotland, where the name was introduced from the Welsh Marches by a family of tenants of the Stewarts in the 12th century.
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, e.g.   Wallach  .

variants.name: Wales
meanings: habitational name from Wales near Sheffield (South Yorkshire). The placename means ‘The Welshmen’ and denoted a settlement of Brittonic speakers living on the boundary between the Anglo-Saxon kingdoms of Northumbria and Mercia (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). See also Wallis .
habitational name from Wales, the country named from Old English wēalas ‘foreigners’ (especially those speaking Celtic or Romance languages). This meaning of the surname is quite rare.

variants.name: Welsh
meanings: Scottish, English, and Irish: ethnic name from Middle English welshe (Old English wēalisc, wēlisc) ‘foreign, Welsh’, for someone from Wales or a speaker of the Welsh language. Compare Walsh and Wallace .
Americanized form of Ukrainian and Jewish (from Ukraine) Voloshin or its Polish variant Wołoszyn (see Woloszyn ).

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Emma Wallis was born in 1495, in Barthomley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Wallis, was 37 and her mother, Katheryn Hayward, was 35. She married John Webster I in 1508, in Cossington, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 20 March 1558, in Cossington, Leicestershire, England, at the age of 63, and was buried in Cossington, Leicestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Emma Wallis est née en 1495, à Barthomley, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Wallis, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Katheryn Hayward, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé John Webster I en 1508, à Cossington, Leicestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 20 mars 1558, à Cossington, Leicestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à Cossington, Leicestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Emma Wallis 1495 in Barthomley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Wallis 37 und ihre Mutter Katheryn Hayward 35. Sie hat 1508 in Cossington, Leicestershire, England John Webster I geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 20. März 1558 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Cossington, Leicestershire, England und wurde in Cossington, Leicestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Emma Wallis è nata il nel 1495 nel Barthomley, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Thomas Wallis, aveva 37 anni e sua madre, Katheryn Hayward, aveva 35 anni. Lei sposato John Webster I nel 1508, nel Cossington, Leicestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 20 marzo 1558, nel Cossington, Leicestershire, England, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolta a Cossington, Leicestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Emma Wallisが1495年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアバーソムリーで生まれた時，父Thomas Wallisは37歳，母Katheryn Haywardは35歳であった。1508年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアコシントンでJohn Webster Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1558年3月20日にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアコシントンで63歳で死去し，イギリスレアイセステルシュアイアコシントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Emma Wallis(이)가 1495년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 바톰리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Wallis(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katheryn Hayward(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1508년 영국 레이스스터실르 코징턴에서 John Webster I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1558년 3월 20일 영국 레이스스터실르 코징턴에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 레이스스터실르 코징턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Emma Wallis nasceu em 1495, em Barthomley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Wallis, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Katheryn Hayward, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com John Webster I em 1508, em Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 20 de março de 1558, em Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, com 63 anos, e foi sepultada em Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Emma Wallis родилась в 1495 г. в местности бартомли, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Wallis было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Katheryn Hayward, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Webster I в 1508 г. в местности коссингтон, лисестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 20 марта 1558 г. в местности коссингтон, лисестиршир, Англия в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности коссингтон, лисестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Emma Wallis nació en 1495, en Barthomley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas Wallis, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Katheryn Hayward, tenía 35 años. Se casó con John Webster I en 1508, en Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 20 de marzo de 1558, en Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultada en Cossington, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.
